summary: Round 11 closes as a hard stop on the filtered_bruteforce_fallback hypothesis. The native authority capture still reached 8897.304 qps at recall 0.95, but the Rust same-schema authority run never completed: after compiling for about 98 seconds, the benchmark process kept running until it was terminated with exit code 143 after roughly 821 seconds. This is a catastrophic authority regression rather than a promotable HNSW gain, so no Rust qps or recall figure is credited for round 11.
action: Keep benchmark_results/hnsw_p3_002_final_verdict.json unchanged, archive round 11 as a hard stop on filtered brute-force fallback, and do not promote this policy into broader HNSW filtered-query handling without a materially different authority-backed hypothesis.
